sections.title: If the player lost the Amulet of the Eye

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that amulet you gave me.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Not to worry. I found it again.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: However, you'll need to make space for it before I can give it to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'll try not to lose it this time.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Any time.

sections.title: If the player still has the Amulet of the Eye or has not unlocked it

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Greetings, adventurer. I am Phileas, the Lumbridge Guide. I am here to give information and directions to new players. Is there anything I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find a quest to go on?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't think of any more quests for you. Don't worry though, there'll be plenty more out there. You'll just need to find them.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I've heard my friend the cook is in need of a spot of help. He'll be in the kitchen of this here castle. Just talk to him and he'll set you off.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I heard that Father Aereck could use a hand. You'll find him in the church just here. Talk to him and he'll give you the details.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I heard that a chap called Veos needs help finding some treasure. Last I heard, he was looking for assistance in the pub just north of here. Talk to him and see if you can help.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I heard that Farmer Fred could use some help. You'll find him on the farm just north of here. Talk to him and see if he needs a hand.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I heard there was some commotion in the swamp to the south. Might be worth looking into.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I believe the Duke of Lumbridge requires an errand running for him. You can find him upstairs in the castle. Just talk to him to get the details.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I heard you were already helping the cook. Are you sure you need another quest?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I heard you were already helping Father Aereck. Are you sure you need another quest?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I heard you were already helping Veos. Are you sure you need another quest?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I heard you were already helping Farmer Fred. Are you sure you need another quest?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I heard you were already investigating the issue in the swamp. Are you sure you need another quest?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I heard you were already helping the Duke. Are you sure you need another quest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want another quest.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I've heard my friend the cook is in need of a spot of help. He'll be in the kitchen of this here castle. Just talk to him and he'll set you off.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I heard that Father Aereck could use a hand. You'll find him in the church just here. Talk to him and he'll give you the details.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I heard that a chap called Veos needs help finding some treasure. Last I heard, he was looking for assistance in the pub just north of here. Talk to him and see if you can help.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I heard that Farmer Fred could use some help. You'll find him on the farm just north of here. Talk to him and see if he needs a hand.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I heard there was some commotion in the swamp to the south. Might be worth looking into.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, I believe the Duke of Lumbridge requires an errand running for him. You can find him upstairs in the castle. Just talk to him to get the details.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I'm afraid I can't think of any more quests for you. Don't worry though, there'll be plenty more out there. You'll just need to find them.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well if you need a reminder, try having a look at your quest journal.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What monsters should I fight?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: It's going to be tough fighting monsters without a weapon. I suggest you find one of those first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I get a weapon?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well you can either make them, find them or buy them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find a weapon?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: If you visit the combat tutors south of the general store they may be able to help you with that. Failing that there are a few cheap weapons lying around in the world. For example, you can get an iron dagger if you

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: cross the bridge and look in the little hut where some goblins live. If it's not there it probably means someone else has taken it. If you wait a while a new one should appear. As you explore you may find other weapons,

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: too.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about something else.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, what would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make a weapon?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: You already have a reasonable Smithing level, so I think you know that already.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: The Smithing skill allows you to make armour and weapons. Talk to the boy who smelts metal in the furnace, I'm sure he can help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I smith?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: You will find a helpful Smithing tutor in the west of Varrock - that's north of here. Follow the path across the river and head north.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I suggest you go and mine some ore; find the Mining symbol - with the guide symbol near it - in the swamp south of here. The Mining guide there can teach you how to mine ore.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I buy a weapon?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: You can get a weapon free from the combat tutors if they think you need it. Failing that, the nearest shop that would sell you something of that nature is Bob's Brilliant Axes in this very town. If you want a bigger

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: choice I suggest you head to one of the big cities. If you follow the road east over the bridge and then head north you will eventually reach Varrock, where you can buy all manner of things. You can use the world map

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: to help you find your way.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about something else.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, what would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I get a staff like yours?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: There is no other staff like this in all the land. It's a very important staff. It shows who holds the job as the Lumbridge Guide, and that's me.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about something else.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, what would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but what should I fight once I have got a weapon?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: There's lots of beasts to fight in the woods around here, especially to the west. There are certainly some goblins and spiders that are pests and could do with being cleared out. There's also a chicken farm or two up the

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: road for some fairly easy pickings. Non-player characters usually appear as yellow dots on your minimap, although there are some that you won't be able to fight, such as myself. A monster's combat level is

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: shown next to their 'Attack' option. If that level is coloured green it means the monster is weaker than you. If it is red, it means that the monster is tougher than you.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Remember, you will do better if you have better armour and weapons and it's always worth carrying a bit of food to heal yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get food to heal myself?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: There are many different foods in the game such as cabbage, fish, meat and many more. Which do you wish to hear about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get cabbages?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: There is a field a little distance to the north of here packed full of cabbages which are there for the picking.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: You could also farm your own cabbages; there's a Farming patch just south of Falador.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about something else.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, what would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I fish?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I am confused. You are already an experienced fisher. You should know the answer to that already.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Fishing spots require different levels and equipment to use. To start Fishing, you'll want to talk to the Fishing tutor who can be found in the swamps south of here. He will also give you a small fishing net if you don't

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: own one already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where could I find one of those?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: You can get them from a Fishing shop or our Fishing Tutor south of here in the swamp. There is a Fishing shop in Port Sarim; you can find it on the world map. Port Sarim is some way to the west of here, beyond

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: the village of Draynor.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about something else.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, what would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find meat?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I suggest you go and kill some chickens. The roads on either side of this river eventually go past a chicken farm. When you have killed some chickens, cook them. You could either make a fire or use a range.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: There is a range at the southern end in this town and a Cooking tutor in south Lumbridge near Bob's Brilliant Axes shop.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about something else.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, what would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, you can make them, buy them or talk to the combat tutors just west of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks, I will go and kill things.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I kill other players?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, you can go to the Wilderness. That is the area for killing other players. Just keep heading north and eventually you will be bound to reach it. Be very careful, though. Player-killing is not for the unprepared.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I'd suggest that you leave any items you don't want to lose in the bank and take a good supply of food with you.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about something else.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, what would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: You're strong enough to work out what monsters to fight for yourself now, but the combat tutors might help you with any questions you have about the skills; they're just over there to the south of the general store.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I make money?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: There are many ways to make money in the game. I would suggest either killing monsters or doing a trade skill such as Smithing or Fishing.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Please don't try to get money by begging off other players. It will make you unpopular. Nobody likes a beggar. It is very irritating to have other players asking for your hard-earned cash.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I smith?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I fish?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find more information?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: What you'll want is the OSRS Wiki! I've taken the liberty of opening the useful links section of the account management tab for you. Here you can find a link to the wiki. There's information and guides on almost

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: everything, all easily searched and it's even written and maintained by players.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know more about security.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: First I must warn you to take every precaution to keep your RuneScape password and PIN secure. The most important thing to remember is to never give your password to, or share your account with, anyone.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I can also tell you about password security, avoiding item scamming and in-game moderation. I can also tell you about a place called the Stronghold of Security, where you can learn more about account security and

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: have a bit of an adventure at the same time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about password security.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, the first thing to remember with password security, which I can't stress enough, is to never tell your password, bank PIN or authenticator details to anyone, not even if they claim to be Jagex staff. Real

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Jagex staff will NEVER ask for your password. Sharing your account is a very bad idea, even if you think that you know and trust the person you are sharing with. Players have lost items and even their

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: account this way. Sharing accounts is also against the rules.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything else to be aware of?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Yes, also be careful where you enter your password. Be aware that fake Old School RuneScape websites exist. These are sites that claim to give things such as item/stat upgrades, free items, beta testing and

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: moderator applications. Jagex do not offer any of these services.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Also be aware of trojans and keyloggers. These nasty programs can send everything you type into your device back to the computers of malicious people, so you can lose more than just your Old School RuneScape

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: password in this way. To avoid getting these on your device, be very wary of what you download, especially when it comes to downloading third party software. Also be very wary of email attachments and instant

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: messenger file transfers, which can contain such things.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Finally, remember the game doesn't censor your password or bank PIN when you type them; anyone who tells you otherwise is probably trying to steal your character or items.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know more about security.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: First I must warn you to take every precaution to keep your RuneScape password and PIN secure. The most important thing to remember is to never give your password to, or share your account with, anyone.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I can also tell you about password security, avoiding item scamming and in-game moderation. I can also tell you about a place called the Stronghold of Security, where you can learn more about account security and

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: have a bit of an adventure at the same time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about something else.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, what would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know more about avoiding item scamming.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: There are many nice and helpful players in Old School RuneScape; unfortunately, as in real life, there are some who aren't so honest. Some people may try to trick you out of your items. Try not to fall for this as

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Jagex policy is to never return lost items to players that have been scammed. Doing so just encourages users to claim they lost items they never had to gain an unfair advantage. However, people carrying out such

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: scams will get banned as it is against the rules.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: When trading, you have a second trade confirmation screen. Always double check that you are getting the items you expect on this page as some people may try and change what they are trading at the last minute on

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: the first trade screen. Remember when you have clicked accept on the second trade screen, the trade is not reversible.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Try and find out from someone else what an item is before spending a lot of money on it. Not all merchants are honest about the rarity or the uses of their wares. For example, some people try to pass off spinach rolls as

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: rare when in fact they're as common as cabbages!

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Be wary of people offering to improve your items. For example, some people will claim they can add a trim to your armour or upgrade your sword to a more powerful one. This is not possible and they are trying

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: to steal your hard-earned equipment.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about something else.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, what would you like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know more about in-game moderation.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: You will, from time to time, see moderator characters in game. Remember: a real staff member will never ask for your password or any other personal information. There are two types of moderator. You may see Jagex

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: staff characters in game - these can be identified by a gold crown next to their name and are the people who work in the Jagex offices. They might be from Customer Support, or they might be a tester or a

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: programmer. You may also see player moderators - these can be identified by a silver crown next to their name. Player moderators are trusted players of Old School RuneScape that help keep in-game behaviour in

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: line with the Old School RuneScape rules.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I become a player moderator?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: The only way to become a player moderator is to know and play by the rules laid down by Jagex. Report those who break the rules, help players who need it and one day you might find an invite to become a moderator in

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: your Old School RuneScape inbox. Note that Jagex will never contact you in game or by email to become a player moderator.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to know about the Stronghold of Security.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: An exciting new discovery has been made in the Barbarian Village. Soon after they started mining in the middle of the village, one of the miners got caught in a cave.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds painful, did he fall on his head?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: No, but it turned out that he had discovered a place unlike any other.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well an explorer went to look down there, it's full of new and exciting creatures and other surprises on top of that. It turns out this place can also help you to secure your account more effectively.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that sounds good. Where can I find the explorer to learn more?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Sadly the explorer went missing and has not been found. However, here is some information to get you started on securing your account. You can find the Barbarian Village north of Falador and west of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find a bank?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: You'll find a bank upstairs in Lumbridge Castle - go right to the top.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Is there anything else you need help with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I can find things myself thank you.

sections.title: Contacting through NPC Contacts Random option

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: What, who's that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hehe... ahem...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is the voice of your conscience! You've been a very naughty boy haven't you?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: What? No I haven't!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know what I'm talking about! Don't lie!

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Okay, okay! I'm sorry, I'll never do it again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you do it again, you'll be in deep trouble.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Sorry but you haven't solved the puzzle yet.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Thanks [player name]!

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I'm supposed to be the local source of knowledge, but I cannot even solve this puzzle! Do me a favour and solve it for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does the name 'Philly' mean anything to you by chance?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: That locket! Where did you get it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found it in a magical rift. Does it belong to you?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Not exactly... It's a long and sad story.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay, I have time to listen.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well, if you're sure then.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: It all started in my youth when I was part of the Order of Wizards. I frequently spent time travelling across Gielinor, which kept me away from my beloved who worked here in Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Due to being apart, I would often send her gifts. This locket was one of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... I'm not sure I like where this story is going. How come I found the locket in a magic rift then?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: As I said, my research often kept me away from home. During one of my trips away, our house was robbed. My dear Thenia tried to stop them, but they took her life in return. The locket was taken in the robbery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh my... I'm so sorry for your loss.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I appreciate your kind words, but this was a long time ago. The pain of losing a loved one never fades, but in time you come to live with it.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Anyway, after that I stepped down from the Order of Wizards. I now spend my days offering guidance to those who come through Lumbridge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still curious to know how it ended up in some rift. Do you have any theories?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Well... where was this rift?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In an ancient temple. Most things in the rift came from the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: The Abyss? I wasn't aware anyone had ever been to the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They hadn't until recently, but the Z.M.I. found a way there.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: The Z.M.I.? But could that mean...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Oh, nothing to worry about. Just me being a silly old man. Tell me more about this temple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's called the Temple of the Eye. It was built by Saradominist wizards long ago to house a powerful artefact.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: The Eye?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You've heard of it?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Only stories, of which there are very few. Still, I always believed them to be real. After all, why else would an eye be the symbol of the Order of Wizards?

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Ah, but that reminds me, I was not the first in my family to join the order. This amulet was passed down from generation to generation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That looks just like the amulet that led us to the temple!

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: As soon as you mentioned it, I wondered if there might be a connection.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: The origin of this amulet was something I spent much time puzzling over when I was younger. I'm glad you have answered the question I couldn't.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: I'd be happy to give you this amulet in return for that locket. I have no use for it, but that locket is something which holds a lot of sentimental value to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course you can have the locket.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Thank you for returning this to me. I wish you the best of luck on the rest of your adventures.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Phileas. I wish you luck also.

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: Oh, I see. I guess I can't really force you to hand it over. The offer still stands if you ever change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind then. I have to go.

sections.title: After not giving him the amulet

lines.speaker: Lumbridge Guide
lines.text: You're back. Would you consider giving me that locket? It holds a lot of sentimental value to me. I'll give you that amulet in return.
